THERE IS NO PROVISION IN CHAPTER 11 OF THE DAVIS-STIRLING ACT (THE CONSTRUCTION DEFECT LITIGATION CHAPTER) THAT PROVIDES THAT A HOMEOWNER'S ASSOCIATION CANNOT RECOVER RELOCATION, LOSS OF USE, AND SUBSTITUTE HOUSING DAMAGES IN A SUIT ABOUT A NEW CONSTRUCTION. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT S L ELECTRIC CO'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. CCP 473(B) PROVIDES: NOTWITHSTANDING ANY OTHER REQUIREMENTS OF THIS SECTION, THE COURT SHALL, WHENEVER AN APPLICATION FOR RELIEF IS MADE NO MORE THAN SIX MONTHS AFTER ENTRY OF JUDGMENT, IS IN PROPER FORM, AND IS ACCOMPANIED BY AN ATTORNEY'S SWORN AFFIDAVIT ATTESTING TO HIS OR HER MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR NEGLECT, VACATE ANY (1) RESULTING DEFAULT ENTERED BY THE CLERK AGAINST HIS OR HER CLIENT, AND WHICH WILL RESULT IN ENTRY OF A DEFAULT JUDGMENT, OR (2) RESULTING DEFAULT JUDGMENT OR DISMISSAL ENTERED AGAINST HIS OR HER CLIENT, UNLESS THE COURT FINDS THAT THE DEFAULT OR DISMISSAL WAS NOT IN FACT CAUSED BY THE ATTORNEY'S MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE, OR NEGLECT. THE COURT SHALL, WHENEVER RELIEF IS GRANTED BASED ON AN ATTORNEY'S AFFIDAVIT OF FAULT, DIRECT THE ATTORNEY TO PAY REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES AND COSTS TO OPPOSING COUNSEL OR PARTIES. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORMAL FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT S L ELECTRIC CO'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEMURRER TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IN THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF 2680 POST STREET AND 880-882 PRESIDIO AVENUE HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION ADEQUATELY ALLEGES A CLAIM AGAINST SL ELECTRIC FOR VIOLATION OF STANDARDS FOR RESIDENTIAL CONSTRUCTION ENUMERATED IN CIVIL CODE 869 AND 897 AND THE CLAIM DOES NOT REQUIRE THAT A COPY OF A CONTRACT BE ATTACHED TO THE COMPLAINT OR THE TERMS OF THAT CONTRACT ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT. SL ELECTRIC'S MISJOINDER ARGUMENT LACKS MERIT. THE COURT HAS ASSUMED, AS APPARENTLY THE PARTIES HAVE DONE AS WELL, THAT THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT ARE NOT ALLEGED AGAINST SL ELECTRIC. ORDER SIGNED. DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT S L ELECTRIC CO'S DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT - Defendant SL Electric Co.'s demurrer to the first cause of action in the third amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiff 2680 Post Street and 880-882 Presidio Avenue Homeowners' Association adequately alleges a claim against SL Electric for violation of standards for residential construction enumerated in Civil Code 869 and 897 and the claim does not require that a copy of a contract be attached to the complaint or the terms of that contract alleged in the complaint. SL Electric's misjoinder argument lacks merit. The court has assumed, as apparently the parties have done as well, that the second and third causes of action in the third amended complaint are not alleged against SL Electric. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

Sl Electric Co.S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, If Entered
Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT S L ELECTRIC CO'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment is granted. CCP 473(b) provides: Notwithstanding any other requirements of this section, the court shall, whenever an application for relief is made no more than six months after entry of judgment, is in proper form, and is accompanied by an attorney's sworn affidavit attesting to his or her mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect, vacate any (1) resulting default entered by the clerk against his or her client, and which will result in entry of a default judgment, or (2) resulting default judgment or dismissal entered against his or her client, unless the court finds that the default or dismissal was not in fact caused by the attorney's mistake, inadvertence, surprise, or neglect. The court shall, whenever relief is granted based on an attorney's affidavit of fault, direct the attorney to pay reasonable compensatory legal fees and costs to opposing counsel or parties. The prevailing party is to prepare a formal form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kara Wild, Esq. of Kasdan Weber Turner, Tel. 925.906.9220, for plaintiff and opposing party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTS JIAN CHEN, CHUN CHEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is Denied. Civ. Code sec. 4775 applies to maintenance, rather than new construction. There is no provision in Chapter 11 of the Davis-Stirling Act (the construction defect litigation chapter) that provides that a homeowner's association cannot recover relocation, loss of use, and substitute housing damages in a suit about a new construction. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.
